FROST Guard Settings

Configure protection against OPFS-based SSD timing attacks

Defense Configuration

Maximum bytes a single origin can write to OPFS. Files below this size stay in the OS page cache, preventing SSD contention leakage. Must be smaller than your system RAM.

512 MB

Random noise added to performance.now() when OPFS is active. The FROST attack requires sub-100µs accuracy; any jitter above that destroys the signal.

±100 µs

Allowed Origins

Sites listed here bypass FROST Guard protections. Only add sites you trust that legitimately need large OPFS storage (e.g., browser-based IDEs, design tools, video editors).

OPFS Usage by Origin

No OPFS activity recorded this session.

About FROST Guard

FROST Guard mitigates the FROST attack (Fingerprinting Remotely using OPFS-based SSD Timing), a browser side-channel that lets malicious websites spy on your system activity by measuring SSD contention through the Origin Private File System API.

Read the FROST paper →
